svg.text: Vector Search at Scale: KNN → ANN → HNSW
How vector retrieval goes from O(N) to O(log N) without losing meaningful recall
1. The problem — KNN doesn't scale
K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)
1. Embed every doc and the prompt → vectors
2. Compute distance from prompt to every doc vector
3. Sort by distance → return top-k
Simple, exact, and brutal at scale.
1,000 docs → 1,000 distances
1B docs → 1B distances (1M× slower)
The bottleneck
Naive vector search recomputes distances
from scratch every query. At billion-scale,
that's seconds per query → unacceptable.
The fix
Pre-build a clever index once, then answer
queries in ~log time. Trade a tiny bit of
recall for orders of magnitude more speed.
2. The fix — Approximate Nearest Neighbors (ANN)

text.tspan: ANN gives up the guarantee of finding the absolute closest doc — in exchange for huge speedups.

svg.text: In practice, the docs ANN returns are very close to the true top-k. The vibes are right; the math is approximate.
Algorithms in this family: NSW, HNSW, IVF, IVF-PQ, ScaNN, DiskANN, SPANN. Today we focus on NSW and HNSW.
3. Navigable Small World (NSW) — proximity graph + greedy hops

g.text: c) Connect each node to its nearest few
1. Pick a random entry node = candidate
2. Look at neighbors → jump to whichever is closer to query
3. Repeat until no neighbor is closer → return candidate
Greedy: chooses best local hop, not the optimal global path.
Each hop only checks a handful of
Most paths converge to the right
• Might miss the true closest doc
• Early hops are still expensive in a

svg.text: 4. Hierarchical NSW (HNSW) — multi-layer shortcut

g.text: Pick a random entry, find best candidate in this tiny graph → big jumps

svg.text: Make big jumps early

g.text: Start at best candidate from layer 3, do normal NSW search in this denser graph

svg.text: Close to prompt by layer 1

g.text: Final descent: best candidate from layer 2 → refine in the full graph → return

svg.text: 5. The complexity payoff: O(N) vs O(log N)
Why HNSW wins
Each layer has ~10× fewer vectors,
so big-jump phase costs almost nothing.
Final layer just refines — you arrive
already very close to the answer.
Billions of vectors →
~100 ms latency.
This is the algorithm behind every modern vector DB.
6. Key takeaways & 2026 production landscape

text.tspan: ANN is much faster than KNN at scale • sacrifices guarantee, keeps quality
Proximity graph is expensive to build, but built once and reused for every query
HNSW everywhere (FAISS, hnswlib), plus DiskANN for SSD-backed billion-scale,

svg.text: cuVS/GPU ANN, binary & Matryoshka quantization → 10-30× memory savings, filtered-ANN for hybrid.
